denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 03/23/2026
Cigna Open Access Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100467221          Group #: GRP-1059
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-112104
Claim #: CLM-2026-191128
Date of Service (requested): 03/03/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Central States Medical Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 03/05/2026 for MRI cervical spine without contrast related to Cervical strain after fall; rule out cord injury (S13.4XXA). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: MRI cervical spine without contrast
Diagnosis (as submitted): Cervical strain after fall; rule out cord injury (S13.4XXA)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: Coverage Policy 119 under Cigna Medical Coverage Policy and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This determination is based on a plan exclusion. State coverage mandates do not alter the terms of this plan for this determination.
Our review applied the criteria in Coverage Policy 119 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. A lower level of care is appropriate for Cervical strain after fall; rule out cord injury (S13.4XXA); MRI cervical spine without contrast exceeds what is required. Outpatient management has not been exhausted per clinical guidelines.

The file does not satisfy utilization-management requirements for level of care too high. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director regarding this determination before or during the appeal process., they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100467221) and reference UM-2026-112104. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
Medical Review Unit
clinical_context: The 67-year-old member has Cervical strain after fall; rule out cord injury (S13.4XXA). She has been treated under commercial coverage with MRI cervical spine without contrast recommended as the next appropriate step. ED visit with paresthesias; midline tenderness; X-ray negative. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that level of care too high bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
